title: Delta-class JV-7 escort shuttle

The '''''Delta''-class JV-7 escort shuttle''', simply known as the '''escort shuttle''', was an armed personnel [[Transport/Legends|transport]] manufactured by [[Cygnus Spaceworks/Legends|Cygnus Spaceworks]].<ref name="SC" />

==Characteristics==
A derivative of the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class]] family, the JV-7 was designed to correct some weaknesses in the original ''Lambda'' design. The craft boasted a higher top speed, and maneuverability was also improved. It had 20% more [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] power and a significantly stronger hull than the ''Lambda''-class.

The aft firing arc was covered by a dual [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] turret, making it extremely dangerous to pursue this ship. The JV-7 was also equipped with a pair of [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] launchers, like the related [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]].

==History==
The class was first seen in use sometime around the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]].<ref name="TF" />

Important [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] tended to prefer the JV-7 over the ''Lambda''-class, especially for transport through contested space. In addition to its personnel transport duties, the JV-7 was regularly employed in escort duties, earning it the classification of "escort shuttle."<ref name="SC" />

As with many craft of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], the JV-7 eventually fell into the hands of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] and some [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] groups. They were used by the [[Mugaari]], and by both combatants in the [[Sepan Civil War]].<ref name="TF" /> Even the [[Duskhan League]] used these during their reign of terror.

At least one escort shuttle fell into the hands of the Imperial splinter faction known as the [[Restored Empire|"hard-liners"]] stationed on the [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] [[RZ7-6113-23]]. The shuttle was stolen by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Kir Kanos]] during his escape from the hard-liners' ship storehouse.<ref name="ce3.3" />

In [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]], ex-Imperial ''Delta''-class shuttles were being scrapped by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] acquired the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] from a ship of this class on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and gave it as a present to his daughter [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina]] at the [[Jedi Praxeum]]. It was later used by marooned Imperial pilot [[Qorl]] to escape [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] aboard his [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]].<ref name="HOF">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Heirs of the Force]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
In the games that feature the ''Delta''-class, the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class]] was toned down. It lacks the potently deadly rear turret, has less armor and shields, and a slower speed than the ''Delta''. The ''Delta'', however, has made few appearances outside of the games.

Kir Kanos uses a ''Delta''-class JV-7 escort shuttle in [[Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost 3|''Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost'' 3]] but it appears as a [[Nu-class attack shuttle/Legends|''Nu''-class attack shuttle]] in [[Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost 4|''Empire Lost 4'']].
